name: Tierra de Ordás Trail
name_original: La Tierra de Ordás – circular desde Santa María de Ordás
description: Hike the moderate 7.5-mile Tierra de Ordás Trail, gaining 822 feet with panoramic views from Torre de Ordás and diverse forest landscapes.
region_intro: Hiking around Santa María De Ordás offers diverse natural landscapes in the province of León, Spain. The region features expansive dehesas, fertile river lowlands, and vast pastures, alongside dense oak and holm oak forests. Notable natural features include the picturesque Valle de Santa María and the geologically significant Desfiladero de Los Calderones gorge. Best hiking trails around Santa María De Ordás The most popular hiking route is Los Calderones de Piedrasecha, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the unique gorge landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Las Miédolas – circular from Las Omañas, an easy 3.2 miles (5.2 km) path. This trail offers varied terrain and takes approximately 1 hour 32 minutes. Local hikers also love the Tierra de Ordás Trail, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail leading through woodlands, past villages, and across open areas, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Hiking around Santa María De Ordás is defined by dehesas, oak forests, and river valleys, with options for various ability levels from easy walks to moderate excursions. The routes in Santa María De Ordás are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa María De Ordás's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Santa María De Ordás?
A: The region of Santa María De Ordás offers a diverse range of natural landscapes. You'll encounter expansive dehesas (wooded pastures), fertile river lowlands (vegas), and vast open pastures. Trails often wind through dense oak and holm oak forests, and you can explore picturesque areas like the Valle de Santa María. A notable natural highlight is the &lt;strong&gt;Desfiladero de Los Calderones&lt;/strong&gt;, a significant gorge.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Santa María De Ordás?
A: Yes, Santa María De Ordás features several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051257901/tierra-de-ordas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierra de Ordás Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 12 kilometers long and takes around 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. Another easy circular path is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050859432/las-miedolas-circular-from-las-omanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Miédolas – circular from Las Omañas&lt;/a&gt;, covering 5.2 km in about 1 hour 32 minutes.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Beyond the scenic landscapes, you can discover unique natural and historical landmarks. The &lt;strong&gt;Desfiladero de Los Calderones&lt;/strong&gt; is a stunning gorge with geological significance. Within this gorge, you'll find the &lt;strong&gt;Ermita Gruta de Nuestra Señora&lt;/strong&gt;, a hermitage nestled in a grotto. Another enchanting spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Manadero in the Cave of the Doves&lt;/a&gt;, accessible via stairs within the cave. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de los Calderones&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or villages to explore while hiking?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can visit the 14th-century &lt;strong&gt;Torre de Ordás&lt;/strong&gt;, a defensive structure offering panoramic views. Many hiking routes connect charming traditional villages like Villapodambre and Formigones, both featuring interesting churches. &lt;strong&gt;Callejo de Ordás&lt;/strong&gt; is particularly notable for its distinctive church steeple, a preserved old dovecote, and restored mills.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Santa María De Ordás?
A: There are 13 hiking trails available around Santa María De Ordás, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These include 4 easy routes, 7 moderate routes, and 2 more challenging options.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Santa María De Ordás?
A: The trails in Santa María De Ordás are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the tranquil environment, and the opportunity to connect with nature through woodlands, past villages, and across open areas, offering wide-ranging views.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy hiking options. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041085482/los-calderones-de-piedrasecha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones de Piedrasecha&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.0 km trail that explores the unique gorge landscape. Another accessible option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40013842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jus loop from Piedrasecha&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.2 km long and also considered easy.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa María De Ordás?
A: Santa María De Ordás offers enjoyable hiking opportunities throughout much of the year due to its diverse landscapes. While specific seasonal recommendations depend on personal preference, the region's oak forests and river valleys provide pleasant conditions in spring and autumn. During heavy rains, some areas like the Desfiladero de Los Calderones might be impassable, so checking local conditions is advisable.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer adventure, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051387126/cuencas-mineras-and-camino-olvidado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuencas Mineras and Camino Olvidado Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.7 km trail that takes approximately 4 hours 27 minutes to complete, offering a more extended exploration of the region's varied terrain.

Q: Can I find places to explore beyond just hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. In addition to the hiking trails, the region offers other points of interest. The nearby &lt;strong&gt;Pantano de Luna (Luna Reservoir)&lt;/strong&gt; adds another dimension to the regional landscape, providing opportunities for scenic views and relaxation. The charming villages and historical structures mentioned earlier also offer cultural exploration.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Tierra de Ordás Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered easy to moderate, making it suitable for a range of fitness levels. While some sections are paved and have low elevation gain, other parts may require good fitness, especially if you're looking for a moderate run. Overall, it's described as an easy and pleasant route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse mix of surfaces, including asphalt, paved sections, dirt paths, and roads. The route often starts on a road before entering the Santa María Valley on ancient paths, forest tracks, and green paths. You'll also traverse areas dense with oak and holm oak forests, and older agricultural lands on slopes, creating a unique dehesa landscape.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Tierra de Ordás Trail?
faqs.answer: The full circular route is approximately 12 kilometers (7.5 miles) long. On average, it takes about 3 hours and 20 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers captivating views of lush green meadows, extensive oak forests, and holm oaks. Key landmarks include the Torre de Ordás, which provides a splendid panoramic vista of the Luna River Valley. You'll also pass through charming villages like Villapodambre, Formigones (home to the Formigones Church), and Callejo de Ordás, with their traditional architecture and ancient dovecotes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Tierra de Ordás Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends in Santa María de Ordás. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you can usually find parking within or on the outskirts of the village, which serves as the main access point for the circular route.

faqs.question: Is there public transport access to the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Information regarding direct public transport to Santa María de Ordás is limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes connecting to Santa María de Ordás from larger towns in the León province if you plan to rely on public transport.

faqs.question: Is the Tierra de Ordás Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages, agricultural areas, or near livestock, to ensure their safety and respect local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, with blooming flora in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, so an early start is recommended, while winter might present colder conditions, though the trail remains accessible.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike the Tierra de Ordás Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is the Tierra de Ordás Trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its easy to moderate difficulty and varied scenery, the trail is generally suitable for families. The mix of paved and dirt paths, along with interesting villages and viewpoints, can keep younger hikers engaged. However, the full 12-kilometer distance might be long for very young children, so consider shorter sections or an adapted route.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trail, like cafes or accommodation?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through several small villages such as Santa María de Ordás, Villapodambre, Formigones, and Callejo de Ordás. While these are small, you may find local bars or small shops in the main villages for refreshments. For more extensive amenities or accommodation, you might need to look in slightly larger towns within the region.
